stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant large language model.
It is presented via Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its performance and community interest.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformer architectures and text generation tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model from Meta.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its community relevance and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its nature as a transformer-based text-generation model.

stories.title: Anthropic got hit by export rules nobody understands
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic was forced to restrict access to its new AI models (Fable 5 and Mythos 5) due to new export rules.
The restrictions, reportedly from the Trump administration, initially blocked access for all foreign nationals, including US users and employees.
The company spent significant time resolving the compliance issues to restore service.

stories.title: Massive breach spills credentials for thousands of sensitive networks
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A massive security breach has exposed credentials across numerous sensitive networks.
High-profile organizations affected include Oracle, Lenovo, FedEx, and Fortinet.
The breach also impacted a contractor serving NATO, indicating significant national security risk.

stories.title: AI coding agents taught robots how to install GPUs and cut zip ties
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Nvidia is utilizing AI coding agents to teach robots complex physical tasks.
The agents have successfully been trained to perform tasks such as installing GPUs and cutting zip ties.
This initiative represents a significant step in the self-improvement and practical application of AI in robotics.

stories.title: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
stories.author: Lily Hay Newman, WIRED.com
bullet_points: Advanced AI models are predicted to soon possess sophisticated hacking capabilities.
The proliferation of these 'dangerous' AI models is presented as an inevitable trend.
The article signals a significant and potentially disruptive shift in cybersecurity and AI development.

stories.title: Towards an Agent-First Web: Redesigning the Web for AI Agents
stories.author: Sachin Shetty
bullet_points: The traditional Web model, built around human users, is being challenged by the rise of AI agents.
Current web infrastructure (access models, economics) is not designed to accommodate automated AI intermediaries.
Web resistance mechanisms like CAPTCHAs and blanket blocking actively impede the functionality of AI agents.

stories.title: OpenAnt: LLM-Powered Vulnerability Discovery Through Code Decomposition, Adversarial Verification, and Dynamic Testing
stories.author: Gadi Evron
bullet_points: Introduces OpenAnt, an LLM-powered system for automated vulnerability discovery in large codebases.
Addresses limitations of traditional methods (high false positives in static analysis, infrastructure needs in fuzzing) by leveraging LLM semantic reasoning.
Combines code decomposition, adversarial verification, and dynamic testing to improve security analysis scalability and accuracy.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline shown is 'text-generation', indicating a generative AI focus.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also delivers faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance on Apple Silicon.
The integration leverages MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, for optimization.
This is presented as a preview, indicating a significant step toward faster local AI inference on Apple hardware.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Tim Cook says RAM expenses are ‘unsustainable’ and Apple is going to raise prices
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Apple plans to raise product prices due to rising component costs.
CEO Tim Cook cited 'unsustainable' RAM expenses as a primary driver.
The price increases are a direct response to the ongoing memory shortage.

stories.title: Epic wants to let you bring your Fortnite skins to other games
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Epic Games is advancing its interoperable metaverse vision by allowing asset portability.
The feature leverages Unreal Engine 6, signaling a major development in game engine capabilities.
This move allows developers to create games that can utilize assets (like Fortnite skins) across different titles.

stories.title: Sooner than expected? Useful quantum error correction promised for 2028.
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Quantum error correction, a critical hurdle for practical quantum computing, is projected to be useful by 2028.
The article suggests progress is happening sooner than previously anticipated in the field.
The discussion encompasses both advanced quantum hardware and the role of classical computing in the quantum ecosystem.

stories.title: Tesco moving 40,000 server workloads off VMware amid Broadcom's “abusive conduct”
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Tesco is migrating 40,000 server workloads away from VMware.
The move is reportedly due to Broadcom allegedly hiking VMware prices by approximately 175%.
The dispute is being addressed through UK court filings.

stories.title: Leaked financial docs show OpenAI is losing billions of dollars a year
stories.author: greenchair
bullet_points: OpenAI is reportedly incurring massive annual financial losses.
The information stems from leaked financial documentation.
This suggests significant operational costs or challenges in the current AI market.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning.
It is optimized for long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released as an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The update also enables web search functionality within the Claude Code environment.
This suggests an enhancement in the capabilities of local LLM orchestration tools for complex tasks.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local generation on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: VSCO launches Studio Pro mobile photo editing app and plans $500 per year subscription
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: VSCO is launching a new, professional-grade mobile editing app called Studio Pro.
The app introduces advanced features like batch editing and style matching from a reference image.
VSCO plans to monetize the app with a $500 per year subscription, challenging Adobe's market position.

stories.title: Two-thirds of Americans think AI is advancing too quickly
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: A Pew Research poll indicates that 63% of Americans feel AI technology is advancing too rapidly.
Usage of AI chatbots has seen a significant increase, rising from 33% in 2024 to a higher rate in the latest poll.
ChatGPT's specific usage has doubled since 2023, highlighting rapid adoption of generative AI.

stories.title: California says AT&T lied to FCC in attempt to shut off old phone network
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: California is challenging AT&T's actions regarding the discontinuation of an older phone network.
The core issue involves AT&T's petitions to the FCC to override state regulations.
The FCC is currently reviewing these petitions concerning the service shutdown.

stories.title: Human-AI Coevolution Dynamics: A Formal Theory of Social Intelligence Emergence Through Long-Term Interaction
stories.author: Haofan Chen
bullet_points: Proposes a 'Human-AI Coevolution Dynamics Framework' to model long-term social interaction.
Addresses the limitation of current AI systems which model social behavior using isolated components (e.g., emotion, memory).
Aims to provide a unified framework to explain the emergence of stable social relationships and social intelligence in AI.

stories.title: A Technical Taxonomy of LLM Agent Communication Protocols
stories.author: Alois Knoll
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical need for robust communication protocols in multi-agent systems powered by LLMs.
It tackles the current challenge of a fragmented and non-interoperable landscape of LLM agent communication protocols.
The core contribution is the development of a technical taxonomy to classify and analyze these various protocols.

stories.title: Equivariant Graph Neural Networks Improve Optical Spectra Prediction for Materials Screening
stories.author: Mikkel N. Schmidt
bullet_points: The research introduces Equivariant Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to improve the prediction of optical spectra for materials screening.
This addresses limitations in existing surrogate models, which often lack geometric expressiveness or rely on lower-level theoretical calculations.
The work adapts GotenNet for this task, aiming to accelerate high-throughput screening for optoelectronic applications like solar cells.

stories.title: Analysing drivers and interdependencies in European electricity markets using XAI
stories.author: Aidan O'Sullivan
bullet_points: The research addresses the complexity and non-linearity of European electricity markets.
It tackles the interpretability issue of Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) used for price prediction.
The core contribution is combining DNN models with Explainable AI (XAI) techniques to understand price drivers.

stories.title: Leadership as Coordination Control: Behavioral Signatures and the Recovery-Advantage Boundary in Multi-Agent LLM Teams
stories.author: Haewoon Kwak
bullet_points: Investigates the role of coordination control (leadership) in teams of Large Language Models (LLMs).
Compares findings to established principles of team science regarding contingent leadership.
Uses behavioral signatures (e.g., majority lock-in, exploration) to measure when control adds value in multi-agent LLM setups.

stories.title: ProductConsistency: Improving Product Identity Preservation in Instruction-Based Image Editing via SFT and RL
stories.author: Kunal Singh
bullet_points: The paper addresses a key limitation in instruction-based image editing: preserving fine-grained product identity during edits.
Current models struggle with maintaining product features, branding, and textual elements in product-centric scenarios.
The research proposes using Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) to improve identity preservation, noting a lack of specialized datasets.

stories.title: Complementary Attention Head Pruning for Efficient Transformers
stories.author: Gonen Singer
bullet_points: Addresses the deployment challenge of large Transformer models due to excessive parameters.
Proposes 'Complementary Attention Head Pruning' as an efficient compression technique.
Aims to overcome limitations of existing pruning methods like instability and manual tuning.

stories.title: ChronoSurv: A Clinical Pathway-Guided Graph Framework for Multimodal Survival Analysis
stories.author: Theo Di Piazza
bullet_points: Introduces ChronoSurv, a new graph framework designed for multimodal survival analysis in head and neck cancer.
Addresses limitations in existing deep survival models by incorporating clinical pathway guidance to capture temporal structure.
Aims to improve personalized treatment planning by handling the heterogeneous and high-dimensional nature of clinical data.

stories.title: Giskard : Byzantine Robust and Confidential Aggregation for Large-Scale Decentralized Learning
stories.author: Sonia Ben Mokhtar
bullet_points: The research addresses the complex challenge of simultaneously ensuring confidentiality and robustness against Byzantine failures in decentralized learning.
Decentralized learning involves clients training models locally and sharing parameters/gradients with neighbors.
The paper proposes 'Giskard,' a method designed to handle both data privacy (confidentiality) and malicious/faulty contributions (Byzantine behavior).

stories.title: Seeing Before Reasoning: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Shortcut-Resilient Multimodal On-Policy Self-Distillation
stories.author: Zhi Han
bullet_points: Introduces ViGOS, a novel framework to adapt On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical limitation in applying OPSD to MLLMs, where the text reference target can cause the model to rely on 'shortcuts' instead of visual grounding.
The method aims to decouple perception (visual input) from reasoning, leading to more robust and shortcut-resilient MLLM post-training.

stories.title: Analysing drivers and interdependencies in European electricity markets using XAI
stories.author: Aidan O'Sullivan
bullet_points: The research addresses the complexity and non-linearity of European electricity markets.
It combines Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) with Explainable AI (XAI) to improve model transparency.
The goal is to move beyond simple price prediction to understanding the underlying drivers of electricity price formation.

stories.title: IndicContextEval: A Benchmark for Evaluating Context Utilisation in Audio Large Language Models Across 8 Indic Languages
stories.author: Mitesh M. Khapra
bullet_points: Introduces IndicContextEval, a new benchmark specifically designed to test context utilization in Audio Large Language Models (AudioLLMs).
Addresses a key research gap: determining if AudioLLMs genuinely use provided textual prompts (context) or rely solely on pretraining knowledge.
The benchmark covers 8 Indic languages and involves 56 hours of natural speech data, focusing on contextual speech recognition.

stories.title: Human-AI Coevolution Dynamics: A Formal Theory of Social Intelligence Emergence Through Long-Term Interaction
stories.author: Haofan Chen
bullet_points: Proposes a 'Human-AI Coevolution Dynamics Framework' to model long-term social intelligence.
Addresses the limitation of current AI systems which model social behavior using isolated components (e.g., emotion, memory).
Focuses on explaining the emergence of stable social relationships during extended human-AI interaction.

stories.title: Leadership as Coordination Control: Behavioral Signatures and the Recovery-Advantage Boundary in Multi-Agent LLM Teams
stories.author: Haewoon Kwak
bullet_points: Investigates the role of coordination control (leadership) in multi-agent Large Language Model (LLM) teams.
Compares findings to established principles of team science, specifically regarding when leadership is beneficial.
Uses behavioral signatures (e.g., majority lock-in, exploration) and ablation studies to measure coordination value.

stories.title: Sumi: Open Uniform Diffusion Language Model from Scratch
stories.author: Jun Suzuki
bullet_points: Introduces 'Sumi,' a Uniform Diffusion Language Model (UDLM) pretrained from scratch.
UDLMs offer a flexible generation mechanism by allowing any token to be updated at any step, unlike traditional autoregressive models.
The research addresses a gap in the field, as no large-scale UDLM has been previously pretrained from scratch.

stories.title: Beyond Tokenization: Direct Timestep Embedding and Contrastive Alignment for Time-Series Question Answering
stories.author: Hung Le
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical limitation in applying LLMs to Time-Series Question Answering (TSQA): the tokenization bottleneck.
Standard tokenization methods (like BPE) fragment continuous numerical data, leading to the loss of essential time-series information (magnitude, scale, trend).
The proposed solution involves 'Direct Timestep Embedding and Contrastive Alignment' to better integrate time-series data into LLMs.

stories.title: US holds off blacklisting DeepSeek, more than 100 firms deemed security risks
stories.author: giuliomagnifico
bullet_points: The US government is reportedly scrutinizing AI/ML providers, specifically mentioning DeepSeek.
Over 100 firms have been identified as potential security risks by US authorities.
The news suggests increased regulatory and security oversight targeting the AI industry.

stories.title: How we run Firecracker VMs inside EC2 and start browsers in less than 1s
stories.author: gregpr07
bullet_points: The article details a technical implementation for running Firecracker VMs within the EC2 environment.
A key achievement highlighted is the ability to launch browsers in under one second using this setup.
This suggests advancements in lightweight virtualization and cloud performance optimization.

stories.title: Launch HN: Adam (YC W25) – Open-Source AI CAD
stories.author: zachdive
bullet_points: Launch of Adam, an open-source Text-to-CAD platform.
The platform uses AI agents to generate mechanical designs for CAD software.
The core philosophy is treating CAD generation as code (text -> code -> CAD).

stories.title: I built a roguelike whose dungeon master is an LLM running 100% in the browser
stories.author: aipredictions_dev
bullet_points: A roguelike game was developed where the Dungeon Master (DM) is powered by an LLM.
The key technical feature is that the entire LLM interaction runs 100% client-side in the browser.
The article critiques the common practice of AI games relying on external API calls, which incurs token costs.

stories.title: The Context Tax: Why Step 12 Costs 42x Step 1 (Measure It in 40 Lines)
stories.author: alex_spinov
bullet_points: The article discusses a performance inefficiency in agent-based AI systems where the entire transcript is resent for every step.
A specific debugging session demonstrated a significant cost multiplier (42.8x) due to this repeated data transmission.
The concept, termed 'The Context Tax,' highlights a practical, high-cost operational issue in current LLM/agent architectures.
